title: Commented-out code should be removed
messageFormat: Remove this commented-out code or replace it with an explanatory comment.
summary: A comment that holds a disabled statement is dead weight: it never runs, the compiler never checks it, and it drifts out of step with the surrounding code until nobody trusts it. Version control already keeps the old text, so the commented statement adds risk without adding value.

This rule flags single-line comments whose content reads as a discarded statement, recognised by a trailing statement terminator or block brace. Keep comments that explain intent and delete the ones that merely park old code.
